QF.id: XIII Introduction to the Underlying Markets_11

question.text: Which committee recommended the introduction of exchange-traded derivatives in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI set up a 24-member committee under Dr. L.C. Gupta on November 18, 1996 to develop the regulatory framework for derivatives trading in India. It submitted its report on March 17, 1998 recommending derivatives be declared as 'securities'. **The Trap:** J.R. Varma Committee (1998) was the follow-up committee that worked on risk containment measures. Narasimham Committee dealt with banking sector reforms, not derivatives. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch1 and Ch8 cover L.C. Gupta and J.R. Varma committees. Series I and IV reference the same regulatory history for currency and IR derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Know both committees: L.C. Gupta = regulatory framework, J.R. Varma = risk management. Both are frequently tested across all derivative certification exams.
